codeReview.summary: Night endpoint implementation is substantial, but it misses two required behaviors from the feature contract: grant_token-based viewer access and viewer date clamping behavior. There is also a metrics filtering inconsistency for owner requests.

issues.description: Viewer date clamping is not applied to execution. enforcePermissions returns effective start/end dates, but the handler only keeps scope.metrics and continues using the original requested date to build time_range and queries. This contradicts the feature expectation to clamp viewer dates to grant bounds. Related evidence: route.ts lines 143-149 and 157-160; tests also assert 403 instead of clamped behavior at night.test.ts lines 467-477.

grant_token is parsed but never used to establish viewer auth/scoping, so direct grant-token deep-links are not actually supported by this endpoint. The LLD explicitly requires grant_token deep-link support (docs/dashboard-backend-lld.md lines 1371-1372). getResolvedContext only consumes middleware/header context and API-key flow (src/lib/auth/resolve-api-key.ts lines 128-134), and middleware has no grant_token handling.

Owner requests ignore metrics filtering for intraday series: the code always fetches all INTRADAY_SERIES_METRICS for owners, even when a narrower metrics query is provided. This is inconsistent with the query parameter contract of comma-separated metric selection.

sharedStateObservations.observation: backend-worker skill instructions are out of sync with actual project practice: the skill mandates bun-based commands and manual curl verification, while this feature run used npm-based checks only and still marked followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md lines 41, 49, 60-67 require bun commands and manual curl verification; handoff 2026-04-05T06-17-07-895Z__night-view-endpoint__74e1b27b-a474-4247-8884-e95da9b30ca5.json lines 16-32 list npm test/typecheck/lint only; lines 129-130 mark followedProcedure=true with no deviations.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared docs and implementation are misaligned on viewer deep-link auth: LLD documents grant_token-based direct deep-links for view endpoints, but the active auth path relies on middleware-issued request context/cookies and does not process grant_token in middleware.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: docs/dashboard-backend-lld.md lines 1371-1372 state grant_token deep-link support; src/middleware.ts has no grant_token handling (search shows no matches); night route only parses grant_token at line 121 without using it for auth resolution.

summary: Reviewed feature night-view-endpoint at commit fb85c61242c19f4a71905ad2e49de6851bfcae82 using handoff, transcript skeleton, and diff. Marked FAIL due to two blocking contract mismatches: grant_token deep-link support is not implemented and viewer date clamping behavior is not applied in the handler.
